stanza: (Amused itself to death)
"Years later, I saw Bill Hubbard's name on the memorial to the missing at Arras"
(Amused itself to death)
"And, and I... when I saw his name, I was absolutely transfixed
It was as though he was now a human being instead of some sort of niightmarish…
(Amused itself to death)
"And I felt relieved, and ever since then I've felt happier about it
Because always before, whenever I thought of him, I said to myself
'Was there something else that I could have done?'"
(Amused itself to death)
"Put me down, I'd rather die. I'd rather die, put me down..."
"And that always sort of worried me. And having seen him, and his
Name in the register - as you know in the memorials there's a little safe"
(Amused itself to death)
"There's a register in there with every name - and
Seeing his name and his name on the memorial. It sort of lightened my heart, if…
(Amused itself to death)
"When was it that you saw his name on the memorial?"
"Ah, when I was eighty-seven. Ah... that would be the year, ninete... eighty-fo…

wendell.linePrev: So I made my way, and we turned the blinds
wendell.line: And Wendell walked in just in time
wendell.lineNext: To see our secret die, and say:
stanza: With the voice of the Lord ringing in his ears
And the note to his wife that confirmed his fears
He sat down on the edge of the bed
Read the letter again, to see who'd sent it
But it was signed "Forever yours"
He stood up slow, like he'd just been hit
Walked into the kitchen, where his wife was sitting
He said, "My mother called on the telephone
She says she needs some help, so I'll be back in the morning"
And he grabbed his heavy coat
My phone rang while I was watching the news
She said the house was ours to cure these winter blues
So I made my way, and we turned the blinds
And Wendell walked in just in time
To see our secret die, and say:

wendell.line: Now, Wendell Walker was a man of God
wendell.lineNext: But he didn't care much for his sober mind
stanza: Wendell Walker was a friend of mine
We'd stain our teeth in the summertime
And with lips of purple, the winter would roll
Past the boarded windows into our souls
And shake our weary bones
Now this past winter was the coldest in years
It's hard to explain if you've never lived here
But it locks your doors and starts your mind
Thinking in circles just to pass the time
And breaks your weary heart
Now, Wendell Walker was a man of God
But he didn't care much for his sober mind
And when the cold mixed in, he was turned around
Heard the voice of God and the angels sound
A message just for him:

wendell.line: Now, Wendell Walker was a friend of mine
wendell.lineNext: But he married too young in the summertime
stanza: Now, Wendell Walker was a friend of mine
But he married too young in the summertime
Their hearts weren't ripe, so they fell apart
And I found myself with a joyful heart
As our secret lives began
We found our moments in between the hours
When Wendell Walker drove his car to town
But one day he found a letter that I wrote for her
On the top of her dresser, and in his winter mind
He heard the voice of God say:

wallflower.lineNext: When the jelly-faced women all sneeze
stanza: Inside the museums, Infinity goes up on trial
Voices echo, "This is what salvation must be like after a while"
But Mona Lisa musta had the highway blues
You can tell by the way she smiles
See the primitive wallflower freeze
When the jelly-faced women all sneeze
Hear the one with the mustache say, "Jeez, I can't find my knees"
Oh, jewels and binoculars hang from the head of the mule

withdraw.linePrev: And when it finally, the bottom fell out
withdraw.lineNext: The only thing I knew how to do
stanza: I lived with them on Montague Street
In a basement down the stairs
There was music in the cafes at night
And revolution in the air
Then he started into dealing with slaves
And something inside of him died
She had to sell everything she owned
And froze up inside
And when it finally, the bottom fell out
I became withdrawn
The only thing I knew how to do
Was to keep on keeping on
Like a bird that flew
Tangled up in blue

withdraw.linePrev: Keep the child close to your chest
stanza: Someone called Maria's name
I swear it was my father's voice
Saying, "If you stay you'll all be slain
You must leave now, you have no choice"
Take the servants and ride west
Keep the child close to your chest
When the American troops withdraw
Let Zapata take the rest

weariness.linePrev: Left me blindly here to stand, but still not sleeping
weariness.line: My weariness amazes me, I am branded on my feet
weariness.lineNext: I have no one to meet
stanza: Though I know that evening's empire has returned into sand
Vanished from my hand
Left me blindly here to stand, but still not sleeping
My weariness amazes me, I am branded on my feet
I have no one to meet
And my ancient empty street's too dead for dreaming

windy.line: Out on the wily, windy moors
windy.lineNext: We'd roll and fall in green
stanza: Out on the wily, windy moors
We'd roll and fall in green
You had a temper like my jealousy
Too hot, too greedy
How could you leave me
When I needed to possess you?
I hated you, I loved you too

windy.linePrev: Well, it makes its home amid the rocks and the rills
windy.line: Where the snow lies deep on the windy hills
windy.lineNext: And it tells the world, "Why should I wait?
stanza: Well, it makes its home amid the rocks and the rills
Where the snow lies deep on the windy hills
And it tells the world, "Why should I wait?
This ice and snow is gonna melt away"

windy.linePrev: Down the foggy ruins of time, far past the frozen leaves
windy.line: The haunted, frightened trees, out to the windy beach
windy.lineNext: Far from the twisted reach of crazy sorrow
stanza: And take me disappearing through the smoke rings of my mind
Down the foggy ruins of time, far past the frozen leaves
The haunted, frightened trees, out to the windy beach
Far from the twisted reach of crazy sorrow
Yes, to dance beneath the diamond sky with one hand waving free
Silhouetted by the sea, circled by the circus sands
With all memory and fate driven deep beneath the waves
Let me forget about today until tomorrow

windy.line: I used to march down the windy, windy sidewalks
windy.lineNext: Slapping my leg with a riding crop
stanza: I used to march down the windy, windy sidewalks
Slapping my leg with a riding crop
Thinking it made me come off so tough
I didn't smile, because a smile always seemed rehearsed
I wasn't afraid of the bullies, and that just made the bullies worse

windy.line: Seashell eyes, windy smile calls me
windy.lineNext: So I sing the song of love
stanza: 2 ContributorsJulia LyricsHalf of what I say is meaningless
But I say it just to reach you, Julia
Julia, Julia
Ocean child calls me
So I sing the song of love, Julia
Julia
Seashell eyes, windy smile calls me
So I sing the song of love
Julia
Her hair of floating sky is shimmering
Glimmering in the sun
Julia, Julia
Morning moon touch me
So I sing the song of love
Julia
When I cannot sing my heart
I can only speak my mind
Julia
Julia
Sleeping sand, silent cloud touch me
So I sing the song of love
Julia
Calls me
So I sing the song of love for Julia
Julia, Julia

Coyote was jumping straight up and making passes
He had those same eyes just like yours
Under your dark glasses
Privately probing the public rooms

No regrets, Coyote
I just get off up aways

She saw poverty
Breaking up a home
Wounded warriors

what'll.linePrev: Till up grows a fine young cherry tree
what'll.line: When the bough breaks, what’ll you make for me?
what'll.lineNext: A little willow cabin to rest on your knee
stanza: All my bones, they are gone, gone, gone
Take my bones, I don’t need none
Cold, cold cupboard, lord, nothing to chew on!
Suck all day on a cherry stone
Dig a little hole not three inches round —
Spit your pit in a hole in the ground
Weep upon the spot for the starving of me!
Till up grows a fine young cherry tree
When the bough breaks, what’ll you make for me?
A little willow cabin to rest on your knee
What’ll I do with a trinket such as this?
Think of your woman, who’s gone to the west
But I’m starving and freezing in my measly old bed!
Then I’ll crawl across the salt flats, to stroke your sweet head
Come across the desert with no shoes on!
I love you truly
Or I love no-one

what'll.linePrev: And what will you do now, my blue-eyed son?
what'll.line: And what'll you do now, my darling young one?
what'll.lineNext: I'm a-going back out 'fore the rain starts a-falling
stanza: And what will you do now, my blue-eyed son?
And what'll you do now, my darling young one?
I'm a-going back out 'fore the rain starts a-falling
I'll walk to the depths of the deepest dark forest
Where the people are many and their hands are all empty
Where the pellets of poison are flooding their waters
Where the home in the valley meets the damp dirty prison
And the executioner's face is always well hidden
Where hunger is ugly, where the souls are forgotten
Where black is the color, where none is the number
And I'll tell it and speak it and think it and breathe it
And reflect from the mountain so all souls can see it
And I'll stand on the ocean until I start sinking
But I'll know my song well before I start singing

I wouldn't need no sneakers
Well, I'd come and go wherever I would please
And I'd scare 'em by the shade tree
And scare 'em by the light pole

wherever.linePrev: And we could tinker lamps and pots
wherever.lineNext: And I said that we might settle down

And picked fruit down in Kent
And we could tinker lamps and pots
And knives wherever we went
And I said that we might settle down
Get a few acres dug
Fire burning in the hearth
And babies on the rug
She said, "Oh man, you foolish man
It surely sounds like hell
You might be lord of half the world
You'll not own me as well"

wherever.linePrev: I can't be myself and I don't want to talk
wherever.line: I'm taking the cure so I can be quiet wherever I want
stanza: Now on the bus
Nearly touching this dirty retreat
Falling out
6th and Powell, a dead sweat in my teeth
Gonna walk, walk, walk
Four more blocks plus the one in my brain
Down downstairs to the man
He's gonna make it all okay
I can't be myself
I can't be myself and I don't want to talk
I'm taking the cure so I can be quiet wherever I want
So leave me alone
You ought to be proud that I'm getting good marks

wherever.linePrev: Thinking about the things I'll tell you
wherever.line: When you get back from wherever it is that you've gone
wherever.lineNext: But then I remember death is real
stanza: The second time I went to Haida Gwaii was just me and our daughter
Only one month after you died, my face was still contorted
Driving up and down, boots wet inside, aimless and weeping
I needed to return to the place where we discovered that childless, we could bl…
But when we came home, you were pregnant
And then our life together was not long
You had cancer and you were killed and I'm left living like this
Crying on the logging roads with your ashes in a jar
Thinking about the things I'll tell you
When you get back from wherever it is that you've gone
But then I remember death is real
And I'm still here in Masset, it's August 12th, 2016
You've been dead for one month and three days, and we are sleeping in the forest
There is sand still in the blankets from the beach where we released you from t…
When we wake up, all the clothes that we left out are cold and damp just from t…
The ground opens up
Surrounded by growth
Nurse logs with layers of moss and life, young cedars, the sound of water, thic…
The ground absorbs and remakes whatever falls
Nothing dies here
But here is where I came to grieve, to dive into it with you, with your absence
But I keep picking you berries

wherever.line: Come gather 'round, people, wherever you roam
wherever.lineNext: And admit that the waters around you have grown
stanza: Come gather 'round, people, wherever you roam
And admit that the waters around you have grown
And accept it that soon you'll be drenched to the bone
If your time to you is worth saving
And you better start swimmin' or you'll sink like a stone
For the times, they are a-changin'

wherever.line: Told her "Wherever you go, little runaway bunny, I will find you"
wherever.lineNext: And then she ran, as they're liable to do
stanza: We take a walk along the dirty lake
Hear the goose cussing at me over her eggs
You poor little cousin, I don't want your dregs
A little baby fussing over my legs
There is a blacksmith and there is a shepherd
And there is a butcher-boy
And there is a barber who's cutting and cutting away
At my only joy
I saw a rabbit as slick as a knife
And as pale as a candlestick
And I had thought it'd be harder to do
But I caught her, and skinned her quick
Held her there, kicking and mewling
Upended, unspooling, unsung and blue
Told her "Wherever you go, little runaway bunny, I will find you"
And then she ran, as they're liable to do

stanza: This waltz, this waltz, this waltz, this waltz ...
With its very own breath of brandy and death
Dragging its tail in the sea

waltz.lineNext: There's a shoulder where Death comes to cry
stanza: 18 ContributorsTranslationsעברית (Hebrew)EspañolTake This Waltz LyricsLeonard C…
There's a shoulder where Death comes to cry
There's a lobby with nine hundred windows
There's a tree where the doves go to die
There's a piece that was torn from the morning
And it hangs in the Gallery of Frost

waltz.linePrev: And the girls down by the strip-tease shows go
waltz.lineNext: You'll go Waltzing Mathilda with me
stanza: 4 ContributorsTom Traubert’s Blues LyricsWasted and wounded, it ain't what the…
I've got what I paid for now
See ya tomorrow, hey Frank, can I borrow
A couple of bucks from you?
To go Waltzing Mathilda, Waltzing Mathilda
You'll go Waltzing Mathilda with me
I'm an innocent victim of a blinded alley
And I'm tired of all these soldiers here
No one speaks English, and every thing's broken
And my Stacys are soaking wet
To go Waltzing Mathilda, Waltzing Mathilda
You'll go Waltzing Mathilda with me
Now the dogs are barking and the taxi cab's parking
A lot they can do for me
I begged you to stab me, you tore my shirt open
And I'm down on my knees tonight
Old Bushmills I staggered, you buried the dagger in
Your silhouette window light
To go Waltzing Mathilda, Waltzing Mathilda
You'll go Waltzing Mathilda with me
Now I lost my Saint Christopher now that I've kissed her
And the one-armed bandit knows
And the Maverick Chinamen, and the cold-blooded signs
And the girls down by the strip-tease shows go
Waltzing Mathilda, Waltzing Mathilda
You'll go Waltzing Mathilda with me
No, I don't want your sympathy, the fugitives say
That the streets aren't for dreaming now
Manslaughter dragnets and the ghosts that sell memories
They want a piece of the action anyhow go
Waltzing Mathilda, Waltzing Mathilda
You'll go Waltzing Mathilda with me
And you can ask any sailor, and the keys from the jailer
And the old men in wheelchairs know
That Mathilda's the defendant, she killed about a hundred
And she follows wherever you may go
Waltzing Mathilda, Waltzing Mathilda
You'll go Waltzing Mathilda with me
And it's a battered old suitcase to a hotel someplace
And a wound that will never heal
No Prima Donna, the perfume is on
An old shirt that is stained with blood and whiskey
And goodnight to the street sweepers
The night watchman flame keepers and goodnight to Mathilda too

waltz.line: Waltzing Matilda whipped out her wallet
stanza: Waltzing Matilda whipped out her wallet
Sexy boys smiled in dismay
She took out four twenties 'cause she liked round figures
Everybody's queen for a day

waltz.linePrev: As snow gathers like bolts of lace
waltz.lineNext: You know, it never has been easy
stanza: I see something of myself in everyone
Just at this moment of the world
As snow gathers like bolts of lace
Waltzing on a ballroom girl

wicker.linePrev: And I can recall our caravel:
wicker.lineNext: With four fine masts and lateen sails
stanza: We sailed away on a winter's day
With fate as malleable as clay
But ships are fallible, I say
And the nautical, like all things, fades
And I can recall our caravel:
A little wicker beetle shell
With four fine masts and lateen sails
Its bearings on Cair Paravel

woof.lineNext: And a thimble's worth of milky moon
stanza: The sight of bridges and balloons
Makes calm canaries irritable;
They caw and claw all afternoon:
"Catenaries and dirigibles
Brace and buoy the living-room—
A loom of metal, warp—woof—wimble"
And a thimble's worth of milky moon
Can touch hearts larger than a thimble

willie.line: 2 ContributorsOur Song LyricsI saw Willie Mays

At a Scottsdale Home Depot
Looking at Garage Door Springs
At the far end of the 14th row

And so, dear friends
You'll just have to carry on

warning.line: Well, they give me all kinds of warnings
stanza: People say I'm crazy
Doin' what I'm doin'
Well, they give me all kinds of warnings
To save me from ruin
When I say that I'm okay
Well, they look at me kinda strange
"Surely you're not happy now?
You no longer play the game"

watchin.linePrev: You're no longer on the ball"
watchin.line: I'm just sittin' here watchin' the wheels go 'round and 'round
watchin.lineNext: I really love to watch them roll
stanza: I'm just sittin' here watchin' the wheels go 'round and 'round
I really love to watch them roll
No longer ridin' on the merry-go-round
I just had to let it go

watchin.linePrev: This is all I think of
watchin.line: Watchin' all our friends fall in and out of Old Paul's, this is my idea of fun
stanza: Singin' in the old bars, swinging with the old stars
Livin' for the fame
Kissin' in the blue dark, playing pool and wild darts
Video games
He holds me in his big arms, drunk, and I am seeing stars
This is all I think of
Watchin' all our friends fall in and out of Old Paul's, this is my idea of fun
Playin' video games

watchin.linePrev: When I tell 'em that I'm doin' fine
watchin.lineNext: "Don't you miss the big time, boy?
stanza: People say I'm lazy
Dreamin' my life away
Well, they give me all kinds of advice
Designed to enlighten me
When I tell 'em that I'm doin' fine
Watchin' shadows on the wall
"Don't you miss the big time, boy?
You're no longer on the ball"

whereabouts.lineNext: Play some Al Green and show her some lovin'

Don't let anyone know bout the whereabouts
Play some Al Green and show her some lovin'
Maybe start drawin', paintin' bout really nothin'

Head swivelin', Question pivotin'
Brought to you by my pop's influence

weaker.line: They would cut a weaker man's neck
weaker.lineNext: While the heroine dressed in a virgin white dress
stanza: The mast is cracking as the waves are slapping
Sailors roll across the deck
And when they thought no one was looking
They would cut a weaker man's neck
While the heroine dressed in a virgin white dress
Tried to steer the mighty ship
But the raging storm wouldn't hear of it
They were in for a long trip

weaker.line: I think he thinks it makes me weaker, but it only ever makes me strong
weaker.lineNext: I've got this friend who sounds just like him
stanza: Thinking he knew where I was going
He put Ryan Adams on
I think he thinks it makes me weaker, but it only ever makes me strong

weaken.line: Would you watch my body weaken
stanza: Night after night
Day after day
Would you watch my body weaken
My mind drift away?

weaken.linePrev: And then leaning on your window sill, he'll say one day you caused his will
weaken.line: To weaken with your love and warmth and shelter
weaken.lineNext: And then taking from his wallet an old schedule of trains, he'll say
stanza: And then leaning on your window sill, he'll say one day you caused his will
To weaken with your love and warmth and shelter
And then taking from his wallet an old schedule of trains, he'll say
"I told you when I came I was a stranger
I told you when I came I was a stranger"

wick.linePrev: I can't give up that quick
wick.line: My life is a candle and a wick
wick.lineNext: You can put it out, but you can't break it down
stanza: I can't give up that quick
My life is a candle and a wick
You can put it out, but you can't break it down
In the end we are waiting to be lit

partner.line: I can't give up that quick

warrior.lineNext: From the Land of the Midnight Sun
stanza: Roland was a warrior
From the Land of the Midnight Sun
With a Thompson gun for hire
Fighting to be done
The deal was made in Denmark
On a dark and stormy day
So he set out for Biafra
To join the bloody fray

warrior.line: Who is a witness to, who is a warrior
warrior.lineNext: Who denies his urge to break and run
stanza: For the mother's restless son
Who is a witness to, who is a warrior
Who denies his urge to break and run
Who says: hard times? I'm used to them
The speeding planet burns, I'm used to that
My life's so common it disappears
And sometimes even music
Cannot substitute for tears
She saw poverty
Breaking up a home
Wounded warriors
Took her song away

weapon.linePrev: I'm guided by this birthmark on my skin
weapon.line: I'm guided by the beauty of our weapons
weapon.lineNext: First we take Manhattan, then we take Berlin
stanza: I'm guided by a signal in the heavens
I'm guided by this birthmark on my skin
I'm guided by the beauty of our weapons
First we take Manhattan, then we take Berlin

partner.line: I'm guided by a signal in the heavens

weapon.linePrev: May we never be called to handle
weapon.line: All the weapons of war we despise
weapon.lineNext: I'll hold you for as long as you like
stanza: Long live all of us crazy soldiers
Who were born under calico skies
May we never be called to handle
All the weapons of war we despise

weapon.line: Weapons of war, symptoms of madness
weapon.lineNext: Don't let your eyes refuse to see
stanza: So now we see how it is
As fist begets this spear
Weapons of war, symptoms of madness
Don't let your eyes refuse to see
Don't let your ears refuse to hear
You ain't never gonna to shake this sense of sadness

williams.linePrev: I said to Hank Williams, ''How lonely does it get?''
williams.lineNext: But I hear him coughing all night long
stanza: I said to Hank Williams, ''How lonely does it get?''
Hank Williams hasn't answered yet
But I hear him coughing all night long
Oh, a hundred floors above me
In the Tower of Song

williams.line: I said to Hank Williams, ''How lonely does it get?''
stanza: I said to Hank Williams, ''How lonely does it get?''
Hank Williams hasn't answered yet
But I hear him coughing all night long
Oh, a hundred floors above me
In the Tower of Song
We asked for signs
The signs were sent
The birth betrayed
The marriage spent
Yeah, the widowhood
Of every government
Signs for all to see

wince.linePrev: If he was fire, oh then she must be wood
wince.line: I saw her wince, I saw her cry
wince.lineNext: I saw the glory in her eye
stanza: I saw her wince, I saw her cry
I saw the glory in her eye
Myself, I long for love and light
But must it come so cruel, and oh so bright?

weekender.linePrev: Just a perfect day, problems all left alone
weekender.line: Weekenders on our own, it's such fun
weekender.lineNext: Just a perfect day, you made me forget myself
stanza: Just a perfect day, problems all left alone
Weekenders on our own, it's such fun
Just a perfect day, you made me forget myself
I thought I was someone else, someone good

wholly.linePrev: From off of the Formica topped bar
wholly.line: And cascading slowly, he lifted her wholly and boldly
stanza: And cascading slowly, he lifted her wholly and boldly
Out of this world
And despite people's derision proved to be more than diversion
And sha la la la la later on

wilshire.line: Pedro lives out of the Wilshire Hotel
wilshire.lineNext: He looks out a window without glass
stanza: Pedro lives out of the Wilshire Hotel
He looks out a window without glass
And the walls are made of cardboard, newspapers on his feet
And his father beats him because he's too tired to beg

wilshire.linePrev: The TV whores are calling the cops out for a suck
wilshire.line: And back at the Wilshire, Pedro sits there dreaming
wilshire.lineNext: He's found a book on magic in a garbage can
stanza: And back at the Wilshire, Pedro sits there dreaming
He's found a book on magic in a garbage can
He looks at the pictures and stares up at the cracked ceiling
"At the count of 3," he says, "I hope I can disappear"

ward.linePrev: A diamond crucifix in his ear
ward.line: Is used to help ward off the fear
ward.lineNext: That he has left his soul in someone's rented car
stanza: Caught between the twisted stars
The plotted lines the faulty map
That brought Columbus to New York
Betwixt between the East and West
He calls on her wearing a leather vest
The earth squeals and shudders to a halt
A diamond crucifix in his ear
Is used to help ward off the fear
That he has left his soul in someone's rented car
Inside his pants he hides a mop
To clean the mess that he has dropped
Into the life of lithesome Juliette Bell

warnin.linePrev: This cop who died in Harlem
warnin.line: You think they'd get the warnin'
warnin.lineNext: I was dancing when I saw his brains run out on the street
stanza: Outside the streets were steaming
The crack dealers were dreaming
Of an Uzi someone had just scored
I betcha I could hit that light
With my one good arm behind my back
Says little Joey Diaz
Brother give me another toke
Those downtown hoods are no damn good
Those Italians need a lesson to be taught
This cop who died in Harlem
You think they'd get the warnin'
I was dancing when I saw his brains run out on the street

wino.linePrev: And we'd sit out on the screen porch
stanza: Mother liked her white wine
She'd have a glass or three
And we'd sit out on the screen porch
White winos mom and me
We'd talk about her childhood
Recap my career
When we got to my father
That was when I'd switch to beer
We got to the old man
And I'd always switch to...

wino.line: 3 ContributorsWhite Winos LyricsMother liked her white wine
stanza: 3 ContributorsWhite Winos LyricsMother liked her white wine
When she was alive
She was desperate to live
But her limit was five
Carefully I'd kiss her
And send her off to bed
We always stuck with white wine
We stayed away from red
Always stick with white wine
Stay away from...
